commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.7.3: the turntable — a specimen glyph scrubbed frame by frame

Rethink the frame-sequence node from scratch (the old painter's plain
colored rectangles were rejected by bmf). The canvas now turns the printer's
showpiece glyph — the ampersand — on a Y-axis spindle, QUANTIZED into 48
pre-rendered frames: the integer frame, not raw scroll, drives the picture,
so the form's defining fact (scroll is an index into a finite sequence) is
real and visible — a flip-book, not a tween.

A film ribbon with sprocket perforations lights up frame-by-frame to the
playhead, and a tracked-mono catalogue label reads the real frame index
(frame 24/48 - turntable) — an honest readout, nothing fabricated. Past 90
the negative cos scale mirrors the glyph automatically (a pale jade-lit
turned page); a confident jade edge sliver carries the thin edge-on moment.

Wears the gallery's shared specimen frame (matte jade crop marks + mono
catalogue label, no bloom) for the cohesive through-line; its mechanic (the
only canvas turntable + film strip) duplicates no sibling. paint(p,n) stays
pure scroll-driven — no timers/WAAPI — so it scrubs cleanly and needs no
extra reduced-motion gating. Font set via the canvas font string (Georgia),
sidestepping the serif-quote silent-fallback trap.

Verified by eye in Chrome across the full scroll range (frames 01..48):
front face, edge-on, back face, terminal; zero console errors (only the
environmental favicon 404).

mit-styling-aj1.7.2: the deck — a flip-stack of letterpress specimen cards (#25)

Reskin the Forms gallery's `deck` node (scrollytelling-inception/index.html)
into a stack of print-specimen PLATES that wears the gallery's shared
type-specimen vocabulary while keeping its OWN distinct mechanic.

Each step-card is a matte plate framed with jade registration crop marks
(a single .dk-card::before pseudo painting 8 strips) + a tracked mono
catalogue label, no bloom — echoing the cascade title's specimen frame
(aj1.7.1). Its distinct demo mechanic: each of the three plates is set in a
DIFFERENT real type family (grotesque / mono / serif), so the stack performs
the movement's thesis 'the same idea, wearing many faces' — same structural
step, three faces — without duplicating cascade's glyph-audition.

- Honest readout: the catalogue label derives wholly from real structure —
  plate index/total + the actual font-family name. Nothing fabricated.
- paint(p,n) stays the pure scroll-driven 3D lift (no timers/WAAPI); the
  face per plate is static identity, so nothing needs REDUCE_MOTION gating
  beyond the global keyframe kill.
- Fixed a silent fallback: the serif family string carries double quotes,
  which broke an inline style="..." attribute and left the serif plate on
  the default face. Family is now set in JS alongside textContent.

Verified in Chrome across the deck's full scroll range: cards lift cleanly
01 grotesque -> 02 mono -> 03 serif terminal; no page console errors.
